findings.rubricLine: 1b (rubric-wave1.md:19-21): http:// allowed ONLY for localhost/127.*/::1; adversarial http://127.0.0.1.evil.com must be rejected
findings.finding: probe_url_allowed classifies any host whose lowercased string merely STARTS WITH "127." as loopback, so http://127.0.0.1.evil.com (and http://127.foo) is treated as loopback and the https gate is skipped. probe_provider (lib.rs:4576) then sends `Authorization: Bearer <key>` over plaintext HTTP to a hostname the attacker controls via DNS (a wildcard A-record resolving *.evil.com to an attacker IP), leaking the provider API key in cleartext. This is a genuine non-loopback host, so it defeats the exact leak the fix's own doc comment (lib.rs:4530) claims to close. valid_base_url does NOT catch it (it only blocks 169.254.*/metadata IPs), and probe_url_tests never exercises the 127.x.evil.com prefix, so the gap ships untested.
findings.fix: Parse the host as an IP and use is_loopback() instead of a string prefix: let loopback = hl == "localhost" || host.parse::<std::net::Ipv4Addr>().map(|ip| ip.is_loopback()).unwrap_or(false) || host.parse::<std::net::Ipv6Addr>().map(|ip| ip.is_loopback()).unwrap_or(false); (host already has [] stripped). Add probe_url_tests asserting http://127.0.0.1.evil.com and http://127.foo are Err.

findings.rubricLine: 1f (rubric-wave1.md:29-30): rollback restores exact pre-call keyring state — no orphan slots
findings.finding: On the FIRST add to a provider that has a legacy key, append_key_txn writes the migrated legacy value to slot 0 (line 4044) then writes the new key to slot new_slot (line 4054), both via `?`. If the slot-0 set SUCCEEDS but the new-slot set FAILS, the `?` returns early and skips the rollback match arm (4064-4071), leaving an orphan provider:{id}:0 (a duplicate of the legacy secret) while JSON keyCount stays 0 — pre-call keyring state is not exactly restored. This is the 'set()-succeeds-but-orphan' path the adversarial prompt asks about. Impact is minor: legacy provider:{id} stays intact so no key is lost, and the orphan is overwritten on the next successful add. The write-closure (JSON) failure path IS rolled back correctly and is the only path the unit test covers; this set-then-set-fail path and the non-first-add path are untested.
findings.evidence:
let new_slot = count;
    set(&format!("provider:{id}:{new_slot}"), key)?;  // early-returns without del(provider:{id}:0) after the migration set at 4044 succeeded
findings.fix: On failure of the new-slot set, delete the migrated slot before returning: if let Err(e) = set(&format!("provider:{id}:{new_slot}"), key) { if migrated { del(&format!("provider:{id}:0")); } return Err(e); }. Add a txn test whose `set` fails on its 2nd invocation and assert no provider:acme:0 orphan remains.

attempts: env KEY metachar (not value): codex_mcp_add_args pushes the whole `format!("{k}={val}")` element (lib.rs:6602) and cmd_argv_safe (lib.rs:6624-6626) scans EVERY char of EVERY argv element, so a metachar in the key rejects the element — key IS guarded, not just the value.
server name: name sits at argv[2] (lib.rs:6597) and is scanned like every other element.
%VAR% expansion: '%' is in the UNSAFE set, so any element with '%' is rejected.
! delayed expansion: outside the frozen set (contracts cmdMetacharSet) and inert under `cmd /C` with no /V:ON — not an injection vector.
newline: a bare LF is not a cmd command separator (separators are & | && ||), so it cannot start a second command through `cmd /C`.
surfacing: rejection is NOT silent — errs.push(err.mcp_unsafe_chars) at 6656 then `return Err(errs.join(...))` at 6673-6675.
http://127.0.0.1.evil.com -> starts_with("127.") TRUE -> wrongly allowed -> KEY LEAK (see finding).
http://127.foo -> starts_with("127.") TRUE -> wrongly allowed (same class).
http://localhost.evil.com -> hl != "localhost" -> loopback false -> correctly rejected.
http://0x7f.0.0.1 -> not localhost/::1, no "127." prefix -> rejected (safe side).
HTTP://api.example.com (uppercase) -> valid_base_url strip_prefix is case-sensitive -> Err(url_scheme), fails closed (no leak).
http://[::1]:x -> host parsed as '::1' -> loopback true -> correctly allowed.
https://169.254.169.254 -> blocked by valid_base_url before the https rule.
Global slot binds to a NAMED `_global` (lib.rs:895), not `_`, so it lives to end of run_forks; Drop always clears FORKS_GLOBAL.
Ordering: flag set at 895 BEFORE the runs-map emptiness check at 896 (preserved).

TOCTOU: ForkRepoSlot::reserve holds the map lock across the FORKS_GLOBAL read (479) AND the insert (485) before drop(m) at 486; run_forks' emptiness check (896) also takes that lock — so run_fork_repo cannot read-flag-false and insert while run_forks reads an empty map. Both can never proceed concurrently.
Double-remove / cross-run remove: each Drop removes only self.path (498-505) after a contains_key guard (482) — a second reserve of the same path is rejected, so no guard owns another run's entry.
Panic-safety: all locks use unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()); Drop runs on unwind.
pid recorded via slot.set_pid(pid) (965) for cancel; both reject paths return err.fork_busy.
Write-failure rollback (the tested path): del(new_slot) + del(slot0 if migrated), legacy provider:{id} untouched -> exact pre-call state restored. CORRECT.
Legacy deleted only after write success (del in Ok arm at 4060) -> CORRECT.
set()-succeeds-but-orphan: slot0 migration set succeeds, new-slot set fails via `?` -> orphan provider:{id}:0 (see low finding).
Non-first-add (count>0): migrated=false, only one set attempted; on its failure nothing is written (no orphan). Byte-identical logic but has no dedicated test.

common.signaturesFrozen: probe_provider, add_provider_key, codex_mcp_add_args all unchanged vs contract.
common.i18n: err.mcp_unsafe_chars (i18n.rs:88, {name}) and err.https_required (i18n.rs:126) present in ru/en/zh.

summary: 1b is a genuine high-severity key-leak bypass -> overall REJECT. probe_url_allowed's hl.starts_with("127.") (lib.rs:4551) treats http://127.0.0.1.evil.com and http://127.foo as loopback, skipping the https gate; probe_provider then leaks Authorization: Bearer <key> over cleartext HTTP to an attacker-controlled DNS name — the very leak the item exists to prevent. Fix with parse-as-IP is_loopback() before merge. 1a CLEAN (env KEY guarded via full-element scan, name at argv[2] checked, % caught, rejection surfaced not silent; ! and newline are out-of-scope non-vectors under cmd /C). 1e CLEAN (named _global guard, flag-before-emptiness ordering preserved, reserve holds the lock across flag-read+insert so no TOCTOU, per-path Drop only, panic-safe). 1f has a LOW self-healing orphan-slot edge on a partial keyring-set failure during first-add migration — worth a follow-up but not a blocker on its own.
